revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: mass importing from wikipedia
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}

{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|t11}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|t11i}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!t11}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!t11i}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects Q]]" to " <noinclude>[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects Q]]</noinclude>"
Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia template redirects Q

revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Documentation}}" to "{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]]"
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23530060143|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24957526818|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Quotation templates | state = {{{state|{{{1|}}}}}} | title = {{c|Quotation templates}} | liststyle = font-size:110%; | tracking = no | list1 = {{Aligned table |class=sortable |style=margin:0 auto 0.4em;line-height:1.35em;text-align:left; |colstyle=padding-right:1.0em; |row1style=line-height:1.6em;font-weight:bold;border-bottom:1px solid #aaa; | Template  | Use{{\}}description  | ↓ ''Block quotations'' | ''General purpose'' | {{tlb|Blockquote}} | Our standard block quotation template: unbordered page-wide quotation with optional smaller-sized attribution | {{tlb|Break lines}} | Transforms line breaks into <br /> tags. It is very similar to the {{tl|poem}} template, except that it does not invoke the <poem> MediaWiki extension. | {{tlb|Numbered verses}} | Quote poems showing the verse numbers | {{tlb|Poem}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); requires substitution | {{tlb|Poem quote}} | {{tl|Blockquote}} variant for use with poems, song lyrics, and other things that would otherwise require the use of {{xtag|poem}} tags or frequent formatting elements (such as {{code|lang=html|code=
}}); does not require substitution | {{tlb|Poetically break lines}} (shortcut: {{tlb|pbl}}) | Designed to format poetry simply and reliably; it differs from {{tl|Poem quote}} in two significant ways: it does not add spacing around the poem that sets it apart as “block quote”, and it automatically provides hanging indentation when lines are so long that they wrap | {{tlb|Translated blockquote}} | Displays original language above and translated text below | {{tlb|Text and translation}} | Displays original language and translated text, one beside the other | {{tlb|Verse translation}} | Displays ''original language verse'' (defaults to italics) and translated verse (roman) side-by-side | {{tlb|Verse transliteration-translation}} | Like {{tl|Verse translation}}, but with a transliteration separate from the original version | {{tlb|Quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|qi}}) | Inline quotation | {{tlb|Cquote}} | "centered quote" – Page-wide quote, between large quotation marks | {{tlb|Rquote}} | "reduced quote" – Quote between large quotation marks in a reduced [[Cascading Style Sheets#Float and clear|float]]ing area | {{tlb|Quote frame}} | Page-wide bordered quote | {{tlb|Quote box}} | Quote floated in a reduced bordered box | {{tlb|Tweet}} | For quoting directly from tweets. Automatically generates {{tlx|Cite tweet}} citation, or a custom reference can be provided. |  |  | ↓ ''Talk page quoting'' | ''Not for use in articles nor the project space at all'' | {{tlb|Talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|tqq}}, {{tlb|tqi}}, or {{tlb|tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Not talk quote inline}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|!tq}},{{Clear}}{{tlb|!tqq}}, {{tlb|!tqi}}, or {{tlb|!tqqi}}) | Inline quotation for what someone ''didn't say'' but could have, or perhaps should have, said. | {{tlb|Talk quote block}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqb}}, {{tlb|tq2}}) | Block-style quotation for use on talk pages | {{tlb|Talk quote block minimalist}}{{Clear}}(shortcut: {{tlb|tqbm}}) | [[:en:Minimalist|Minimalist]] version of above |  |  | ↓ ''Alternative'' | ''For indenting non-quotations'' | {{tlb|Block indent}} | To indent an entire block of content (uses {{code|lang=html|code=
}} and CSS margins) | {{tlb|Divbox}} | To frame messages or the example output of an article or template }} }}{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
